answer:
**The work is the *Gaṇapatitattva* (by Unknown), Chapter 1.**[^1]

The two ślokas describing the five-syllable mantra *Pañcāṇḍa* (also spelled *Pañcakāṇḍa*) and its precise mapping to the five gross elements (*earth* to *jaḥ*, *waters* to *taḥ*, *fire* to *kiḥ*, *wind* to *uṃ*, *ether* to *phaṭ*)—to be recited for the destruction of great sins and to grant the wise reciter the world of Śiva—are as follows:

> In the syllable *jaḥ*, earth is to be known; in the syllable *taḥ*, the waters are established.  
> In the syllable *kiḥ* is the great fire; in the syllable *uṃ*, one should place the wind. // (54)[^2]

> Joined with the syllable *phaṭ* for the ether, for the destruction of great sins.  
> He who, being wise, recites the *Pañcāṇḍa*, attains the world of Śiva. // (55)[^3]

[^1]: *Gaṇapatitattva*, [Chapter 1](/works/unknown-ganapatitattva-66a136/01/translation/).
[^2]: *Gaṇapatitattva*, [paragraph](/works/unknown-ganapatitattva-66a136/01/translation/#p-570c4a).
[^3]: *Gaṇapatitattva*, [paragraph](/works/unknown-ganapatitattva-66a136/01/translation/#p-bb1220).
